# CDviz

> CDviz is an open-source event-driven CI/CD platform built on the CDEvents standard. It collects continuous delivery events from CI/CD tools, stores them in PostgreSQL with TimescaleDB, visualizes them in Grafana dashboards, and can trigger automated workflows from the same event stream. It follows an "observe before act" philosophy — gain visibility into your pipelines before automating decisions.

CDviz is developed by Alchim312. The platform is licensed under Apache License v2. A SaaS option is planned — currently accepting waitlist signups at <https://cdviz.dev/pricing>. Development will be prioritized based on interest.

## Core Components

- [CDviz Collector](https://cdviz.dev/docs/cdviz-collector/): Event collection agent that ingests webhooks, Kafka, NATS, SSE, and file-based events, normalizes them to CDEvents, and routes to configured sinks. Written in Rust.
- [CDviz Database](https://cdviz.dev/docs/cdviz-db/): PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB schema for storing CDEvents as a time-series hypertable with JSONB payloads. Uses golang-migrate for schema evolution.
- [CDviz Grafana](https://cdviz.dev/docs/cdviz-grafana/): TypeScript-generated Grafana dashboards (Grafana Foundation SDK) for deployment tracking, DORA metrics, artifact timelines, and CDEvents activity feeds.

## Key Concepts

- CDEvents is a CD Foundation (CDF) initiative defining a standard event format for continuous delivery pipelines. CDviz is built natively on CDEvents.
- CDviz differs from Apache DevLake (polling-based, proprietary model) by using real-time event push and the open CDEvents standard.
- CDviz differs from Datadog CI Visibility by being self-hosted, open-source, and owning your data.
- CDviz supports both passive monitoring (via webhook transformers for GitHub, GitLab, ArgoCD) and active integration (send-cdevents GitHub Action).

## Use Cases

- Track DORA metrics (deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, time to restore) without a commercial tool
- Monitor CI/CD pipeline performance and test flakiness across environments
- Replace homegrown internal dashboards built on GitLab or GitHub data
- Visualize CDEvents across a multi-tool software delivery pipeline
- Trigger automated workflows from deployment or test events via CDEvents

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](https://cdviz.dev/docs/getting-started): Local demo setup with Docker Compose
- [Architecture](https://cdviz.dev/docs/architecture): Collector → Database → Grafana → Event Reactions pipeline
- [CDEvents Standard](https://cdviz.dev/docs/cdevents): CDEvent JSON structure and field reference
- [Alternatives Comparison](https://cdviz.dev/docs/alternatives): CDviz vs Apache DevLake, Datadog CI Visibility, Powerpipe
- [Pricing](https://cdviz.dev/pricing): Open-source (free), commercial subscriptions available

## Blog

- [Episode 0: Monitor Your Software Factory](https://cdviz.dev/blog/20250821-episode-0-manager-problem): Problem statement and CDviz overview
- [Episode 1: Simulate a Consumer](https://cdviz.dev/blog/20250916-episode-1-simulate-consumer): Test CDEvents integration before building
- [Episode 2: Send Your First CDEvent](https://cdviz.dev/blog/20251001-episode-2-send-first-cdevent): Three approaches to send CDEvents from pipelines
- [Episode 3: Direct CI/CD Pipeline Integration](https://cdviz.dev/blog/20251007-episode-3-cicd-integration): Universal patterns to send CDEvents from any CI/CD system
- [Episode 4: Webhook Transformers](https://cdviz.dev/blog/20251020-episode-4-webhook-transformers): Passive monitoring with GitHub, GitLab, ArgoCD
- [Episode 5: The Kubernetes Deployment Blind Spot](https://cdviz.dev/blog/20260311-episode-5-k8s-blind-spot)
- [Episode 6: Monitor Every Kubernetes Deployment with One Helm Command](https://cdviz.dev/blog/20260318-episode-6-kubewatch-helm-recipe): Passive monitoring with Kubernetes

## Optional

- [GitHub Organization](https://github.com/cdviz-dev): Source code for all CDviz components
- [YouTube Channel](https://www.youtube.com/@CDviz): Videos and demos
- [dev.to Articles](https://dev.to/davidb31): Blog posts and tutorials
- [Live Demo](https://demo.cdviz.dev/grafana): Read-only Grafana dashboard instance

## Key facts

- CDviz follows an "observe before act" philosophy: the same event stream used for metrics can trigger automated workflows
- DORA metrics thresholds: Elite (≥1 deploy/day, lead time <1h, restore <1h, failure rate ≤5%) to Low (<1 deploy/month, >1 week lead time)
- CDEvents types tracked include: service.deployed, service.upgraded, service.rolledback, artifact.published, build.finished, pipelinerun.queued, pipelinerun.finished, incident.detected, incident.resolved
- 15+ integration sources: GitHub, GitLab, ArgoCD, Kubernetes (Kubewatch), Kafka, NATS, Jenkins, custom webhooks
- CDviz Collector is written in Rust; dashboards generated from TypeScript via Grafana Foundation SDK
- Zero pipeline changes required for passive monitoring via webhook transformers (GitHub, GitLab, ArgoCD)
- Apache License v2 for all components (cdviz-collector v0.15+, cdviz-db, cdviz-grafana)
